summary: PR #2253 decompiled five Kirby neutral-special-related fighter functions across four ftKirby source files. The PR body claimed clean ninja build and 100% fuzzy match for all five functions; the decomp-dev bot reported five new 100% matches totaling +776 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Implemented ftKb_EatWalk_IASA, including jump-input handling, EatJump1/EatWait motion transitions, and fallback walk-common IASA handling.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported ftKb_EatWalk_IASA 0.00% -> 100.00%, +256 bytes
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim +256 bytes and ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C +80 bytes, both 100.00%

key_files.role: Added ftCo_Bury include and implemented fn_8010B16C under a narrow dont_inline pragma block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.h and fn_8010B16C body
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported fn_8010B16C 0.00% -> 100.00%, +104 bytes

key_files.role: Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C as a motion-id predicate for Mewtwo copied neutral-special states.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C 0.00% -> 100.00%, +80 bytes

what_changed.text: Replaced five placeholder decomp markers with C implementations: ftKb_EatWalk_IASA, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C, ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, fn_8010B16C, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C.
Added an include for ftCo_Bury.h in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c to support the newly implemented fn_8010B16C dependency surface.
Used PAD_STACK in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA and ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, and a scoped #pragma dont_inline on/reset around fn_8010B16C.
Bot verification reported five new 100% matches and +776 matched bytes for GALE01.

smart_moves.text: Kept matching tactics narrow: PAD_STACK appears only inside two new matched functions, and dont_inline is scoped immediately around fn_8010B16C with a reset after the function.
Implemented motion-state predicates as explicit switch statements over known motion IDs or named motion-state constants, returning the x2071_b6 flag only for selected state ranges and true otherwise.

Used typed Fighter and Kirby data accesses such as fp->motion_id, fp->x2070.x2071_b6, fp->fv.kb.hat.kind, fp->dat_attrs, and fp->grab_timer instead of raw pointer arithmetic in the shown implementations.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small copied-character predicate helpers, a null-gobj early true return plus a switch over the copied character's relevant motion states can be a compact matching shape; within selected states, the predicate may reduce to fp->x2070.x2071_b6.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reported both predicate helpers 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching Kirby copied Link aerial neutral-special end animation, evidence from this PR supports cleanup calls to unset arrow and bow before choosing fall/freefall behavior based on Link-vs-Young-Link dat_attrs freefall toggles.

For Kirby EatWalk IASA matching, this PR used ftCo_Jump_GetInput first, stored non-none jump input into fp->mv.kb.specialhi fields before changing to ftKb_MS_EatJump1, and otherwise checked ft_8008A1FC before falling back to ftWalkCommon_800DFEC8.

naming_conventions.text: Local Fighter pointers are named fp and Fighter_GObj parameters are named gobj in the new implementations.
The PR preserved address-style names where semantics were not recovered, including fn_8010B16C and helper references such as ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109380.
The PR used a short dat_attrs local name da in ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A scoped #pragma dont_inline on/reset block was used around fn_8010B16C.
A goto label end_true was used in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C for the null-gobj true path.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: claimed five decompiled functions, ninja clean, fuzzy_match_percent 100.0
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all four changed files
notes: No review comments or human reviewer feedback were loaded for this slice.
changed_files metadata appears duplicated in metadata_json and changed_files.jsonl; unique changed paths are four files.
The PR body claims ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0; bot report independently lists five 100.00% new matches, but no full local build log or objdiff output is included in the slice.
Use of Claude Code is present in PR body; this is evidence of generation note only, not evidence about correctness beyond the included verification and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Scoped dont_inline around a single matched fighter helper
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2253 used #pragma dont_inline on/reset only around fn_8010B16C in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c; the bot report listed fn_8010B16C as a new 100.00% match (+104 bytes).

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK use in two matched ftKirby functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2253 used PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_EatWalk_IASA and PAD_STACK(24) in ftKb_LkSpecialAirNEnd_Anim; the bot report listed both functions as new 100.00% matches.

source_update_candidates.title: ftKirby copied neutral-special predicate helpers matched in PR #2253
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c contains ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C and src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c contains ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C; both were reported as 100.00% new matches in PR #2253.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-fact update for function ownership and location; curator should verify against current source before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK or dont_inline as preferred style from this PR alone; they are narrow matching tactics with limited evidence.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale because no review comments or reviews were loaded.
Do not generalize the numeric motion IDs in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC6C into named constants without additional source or symbol evidence.
